description: AI Physique Analysis - Tab Progresso com check-ins semanais de physique via fotos analisadas pela Claude Vision API

userStories.title: Install dependencies and create Progresso tab route group
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the new dependencies installed and the route structure created so other stories can build on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npm install expo-image-picker expo-file-system @ronradtke/react-native-markdown-display --legacy-peer-deps`
Create `app/(tabs)/(progresso)/_layout.tsx` with Stack layout following the same pattern as `(historico)/_layout.tsx` (headerLargeTitle, headerTransparent, etc) with title 'Progresso'
Create `app/(tabs)/(progresso)/index.tsx` with empty ScrollView (contentInsetAdjustmentBehavior='automatic') and placeholder text
Add new Tabs.Screen in `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` for `(progresso)` with title 'Progresso' and Ionicons icon `analytics-outline` (matching existing Ionicons pattern in that file)
Tab renders and navigates correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create physique check-in Zustand store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Zustand store to persist check-in metadata (no photos in store).
acceptanceCriteria: Create `stores/usePhysiqueStore.ts` following the same pattern as `useConfigStore.ts` (Zustand + persist + AsyncStorage)

Interface `PhysiqueCheckIn`: id (string), week (number), date (string), weight (number), previousWeight (number, optional), notes (string, optional), photoPaths (string[]), analysis (string, optional), mode ('full' | 'comparative' | 'quick')
Hardcoded athlete constants at top of file: ATHLETE_NAME='Gabriel Cruz', ATHLETE_AGE=26, ATHLETE_HEIGHT='1.72m', ATHLETE_PHASE='Cutting'
Actions: addCheckIn(checkIn: Omit<PhysiqueCheckIn, 'id'>) generates UUID-style id and appends to array, updateAnalysis(id: string, analysis: string) updates the matching check-in
State is an array of PhysiqueCheckIn objects
Persist middleware with storage name 'physique-store'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create photo capture component with permissions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to take or select progress photos with proper permission handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `components/physique/PhotoSlots.tsx` component
Receives props: photos (array of {uri, label}), onAdd(index), onRemove(index) — max 4 slots
4 labeled slots: 'Frontal', 'Lateral', 'Costas', 'Extra'
Each empty slot shows a dashed border card with camera icon (MaterialCommunityIcons 'camera-plus-outline') and label text
Tapping empty slot: requests camera/gallery permission via expo-image-picker, shows ActionSheet-style choice (camera or gallery)
If permission denied: shows Alert with message explaining why and suggesting to enable in device settings
Image picker config: mediaTypes 'images', quality 0.8, maxWidth 1024, maxHeight 1024
Filled slot shows image preview with remove button (X icon) in top-right corner
Uses theme.colors for styling (dark theme, amber accent)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create new check-in form screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a form screen to enter weight, week, notes and photos to submit a check-in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `app/(tabs)/(progresso)/new-checkin.tsx` route with formSheet presentation (same pattern as dia-detalhe: sheetGrabberVisible, sheetAllowedDetents [0.75, 1.0])
Register route in `(progresso)/_layout.tsx` Stack.Screen
Uses PhotoSlots component for photo capture
Numeric input for weight (kg) — required, placeholder '83.5'
Numeric input for prep week — required, defaults to last check-in week + 1 (or 1 if first), reads from usePhysiqueStore
TextInput for notes — optional, multiline, placeholder 'Observações: me sentindo flat, retenção aumentou...'
Mode selector: two Pressable pills 'Análise Completa' (default) and 'Quick Check'
Quick Check mode hides Lateral/Costas/Extra photo slots (shows only Frontal)
Submit button disabled until validation passes: weight > 0, week > 0, at least 1 photo
On submit: saves photos to FileSystem.documentDirectory/physique/{id}-{index}.jpg, creates check-in in store with photoPaths, navigates to result screen with check-in id
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create Claude Vision API service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API integration service to send photos and receive physique analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `constants/api.ts` with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY constant (placeholder value, user will fill in) — add this file to .gitignore
Create `services/physiqueAnalysis.ts` with analyzePhysique function
Function signature: analyzePhysique(photoPaths: string[], mode: 'full' | 'comparative' | 'quick', context: { week, weight, previousWeight?, notes?, previousPhotoPaths? }): Promise<string>
Reads photos from filesystem as base64 using expo-file-system readAsStringAsync with base64 encoding
System prompt: full bodybuilding coach prompt from the prompt engineering doc (hardcoded athlete data from store constants)
3 user prompt templates based on mode: full (check-in de progresso), comparative (check-in comparativo with [ATUAL]/[ANTERIOR] labels and peso delta), quick (quick check)
Comparative mode: receives previousPhotoPaths, reads them as base64, includes in message with [ANTERIOR] labels
API call: POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages, model claude-sonnet-4-20250514, max_tokens 4096
Headers: x-api-key, anthropic-version '2023-06-01', content-type 'application/json'
Timeout: AbortController with 60s timeout
Returns analysis text string, throws descriptive errors for network/API/timeout failures
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire submit flow with API call and loading state
userStories.description: As a user, I want to submit my check-in and see a loading state while the AI analyzes my photos.
acceptanceCriteria: In new-checkin.tsx: after saving photos and creating check-in, call analyzePhysique service
Detect comparative mode: if store has a check-in for (current week - 1), automatically use comparative mode and pass previous photo paths (unless user selected Quick Check)
Show fullscreen loading overlay during API call with message 'Analisando suas fotos...' and ActivityIndicator
On success: call updateAnalysis(id, result) on store, navigate to result screen
On error: show Alert with user-friendly message ('Sem conexão', 'Erro na API', 'Timeout - tente novamente'), stay on form screen
Disable submit button during API call to prevent double-submit
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create analysis result screen with markdown rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the AI analysis in a formatted screen with my photos and metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `app/(tabs)/(progresso)/result.tsx` route, receives `id` param via query string
Register route in `(progresso)/_layout.tsx` Stack.Screen with title 'Análise'
Header section: week number, date, weight, delta (if comparative), badge showing mode (Completa/Comparativa/Quick)
Horizontal scrollable row of photo thumbnails from check-in photoPaths (loaded from filesystem)
Renders analysis text using @ronradtke/react-native-markdown-display
Markdown styles customized to match dark theme: bg primary, text primary, heading colors using theme.colors, code blocks with bg-elevated
ScrollView for long analyses
If analysis is empty/undefined (still loading or failed), show appropriate empty state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build check-in history list on Progresso index
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my past check-ins listed on the Progresso tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `app/(tabs)/(progresso)/index.tsx` to show list of check-ins from usePhysiqueStore
List ordered by week descending (most recent first)
Each card (using <Card> component) shows: 'Semana X' title, date, weight with delta if previousWeight exists, mode badge (Completa/Comparativa/Quick), thumbnail of first photo from photoPaths
Tap card navigates to result screen with check-in id
Floating action button or header button 'Novo Check-in' navigates to new-checkin route
Empty state: analytics-outline icon (MaterialCommunityIcons) at 48px + 'Nenhum check-in ainda' + subtitle 'Comece seu primeiro check-in de progresso' + 'Novo Check-in' button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add fullscreen photo gallery viewer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view my progress photos in fullscreen with swipe navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: In result.tsx: tapping a photo thumbnail opens fullscreen modal overlay
Fullscreen view: dark background, photo centered and scaled to fit
Horizontal swipe (FlatList or ScrollView with pagingEnabled) between photos of the same check-in
Label visible at bottom showing photo type (Frontal/Lateral/Costas/Extra)
Close button (X icon) in top-right corner or tap-to-dismiss
Typecheck passes
